title: SpaceX Investors at US Retail Brokers Got at Least One IPO Share
summary: Customers at some of the largest retail brokerage firms in the US each received at least a single share in SpaceX’s $86.2 billion IPO, underscoring how the offering was designed to give individual investors a sizable role.

title: Stock Rally Cools as Central Banks Take Spotlight: Markets Wrap
summary: Global stocks wavered as investors paused to assess the durability of the relief rally fueled by the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, while parsing the latest policy decisions by central banks in Japan and Australia.

title: China’s Property Stocks Tumble Back to Pre-2024 Stimulus Levels
summary: Chinese property stocks have slid back to levels seen before a raft of stimulus measures from the authorities in September 2024 drove a market turnaround, underscoring lingering pessimism over the sector.

title: Aluminum Extends Slump as China Data Adds to Downside Pressure
summary: Aluminum dropped to its lowest since March after weaker-than-expected economic data from China dragged on metals, while investors assessed lingering questions over the execution of a peace deal between Iran and the US.

title: Yuan’s Rally to Three-Year High Is Enough for AllianzGI for Now
summary: Allianz Global Investors has scaled back some of its bullish yuan position and turned neutral, taking profits after a rally that has made the Chinese currency Asia’s best performer this year.

title: FTSE 100 Set to Extend Fall After Missing Rally
BHP and Rio Tinto See India Driving Steel’s Next Wave of Growth
summary: BHP Group and Rio Tinto Plc are looking increasingly to India as the next big growth engine for global steelmaking, underscoring how the world’s biggest iron ore suppliers are preparing for a future beyond China.

title: Oil Suffers Worst Run This Year as Hormuz Deal Reshapes Outlook
summary: Oil headed for its longest losing streak this year as the US-Iran deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz boosted expectations for a revival in supply, with leading Wall Street banks reducing their price forecasts.

title: Bank of Japan Needs Strong Measures to Prop Up Yen: Strategists
summary: Strategists expect the yen and government bonds to remain under pressure while Japan’s stocks continue to find support following the Bank of Japan’s decision to raise rates on Tuesday.

title: Bank of Japan Raises Benchmark Interest Rate to 1%
summary: Japan's central bank increased its benchmark interest rate to the highest since 1995 and signaled that further policy normalization lies ahead. Bloomberg's Shery Ahn reports from Tokyo. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Jardine Matheson to buy back $500M in shares
summary: Lincoln Pan, CEO at Jardine Matheson Holdings, discusses the group's strategic blueprint that includes a share buyback, boosting returns and adding exposure to developed markets. He speaks exclusively on "Bloomberg: The China Show." (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Ayub: Markets Always Look at Trump’s Next Focus
summary: Asian stocks wavered as investors gauged whether the US-Iran peace deal can really sustain the relief rally. Bank of Singapore's Head of Managed Solutions Advisory Dubai, Mehvish Ayub spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East & Africa on the risk allocation of the US-Iran war. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Evergrande Liquidators Seek to Quash Regulator’s PwC HK Deal
summary: China Evergrande’s liquidators have asked a court to void a HK$1 billion ($127.6 million) shareholder compensation deal a regulator struck with PricewaterhouseCoopers’ Hong Kong affiliate.

title: Yen Trims Gain, JGBs Fall on Concern That BOJ Needs to Do More
summary: The yen pared gains against the dollar and Japanese bonds fell on speculation the Bank of Japan may need to move more decisively to give support to the currency and stem inflation.

title: Fahmy: Trump Wants to Reshape the Middle East
summary: European allies remained skeptical President Trump's deal with Iran will lead to the re-opening of the Strait of Hormuz by June 19. The deal is also seen straining Trump's relationship with Israel’s PM Benjamin Netanyahu. Dalia Fahmy, Director of International Relations and Diplomacy & Associate Professor of Political Science at Long Island University spoke to Bloomberg’s Abeer Abu Omar on Horizons Middle East & Africa on the future of the region. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Strait of Hormuz to Reopen by Friday, Trump Says
summary: US President Donald Trump says the Strait of Hormuz will reopen by Friday, but his European allies don't share his optimism. Bloomberg's Dan Williams and Stephen Stapczynski report on what we know so far. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Sify Infinit Is Said to Put $391 Million India IPO on Hold
summary: Sify Infinit Spaces Ltd. has put its planned 37 billion rupee ($391 million) initial public offering on hold, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a growing list of companies delaying share sales because of India’s weak stock market.

title: Wall Street Majors Cut Oil Forecasts on Deal to Reopen Hormuz
summary: Wall Street’s biggest banks are cutting their oil-price forecasts for the coming quarters, as optimism grows over a revival of Middle East crude output following an interim deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

title: AI Re-Rating Fuels 550% Rally in Hong Kong’s Kingboard Laminates
summary: Kingboard Laminates Holdings Ltd. has rallied more than sixfold this year as investors bet the Chinese supplier to the printed circuit board industry will emerge as a key beneficiary of the AI buildout.

title: The Two Clear Winners Are Iran and Markets
summary: The deal hasn’t had much impact as yet on central banks and rate decisions.

title: Vietnam Stocks See Biggest Foreign Inflows in Nearly Six Years
summary: Vietnamese shares saw the biggest daily foreign inflows in nearly six years, in a sign of renewed global investor interest in the Southeast Asian market as the Middle East tensions eased.

title: Middle East Oil Markets Weaken on Optimism Over Increased Supply
summary: Middle Eastern crude markets weakened as the US-Iran agreement to reopen the Strait of Hormuz spurred optimism around an increase in flows from the oil-rich region.

title: China’s Consumer Spending Drop Imperils Growth
summary: China’s consumer spending contracted last month for the first time since the pandemic and investment deteriorated, exposing risks still facing the economy even as it benefits from booming exports and a deescalation in tensions around Iran. UBS Securities Chief China Economist Yu Song shares his analysis. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: China’s Oil Refiners Slash Output After Crude Imports Plunge
summary: Chinese oil refiners sharply reduced output last month to the weakest level in nearly four years, after the country’s crude imports plunged to an eight-year low due to the near-halt to shipments from the Persian Gulf.

title: Jardine to Buy Back $500 Million Shares, Boost Returns
summary: Jardine Matheson Holdings Ltd. will buy back $500 million of its own shares by the end of next year as the Hong Kong company lays out targets for its transformation from a long-term owner-operator into an active investor more akin to a private equity fund.

title: Biocon's Mazumdar-Shaw Says Firm On Track To Be Top Insulin Maker
summary: Biocon Executive Chairperson Kiran Mazumdar-Shaw says she is confident that the company's investment and capacity expansion in Insulin will make the company the top hub for the drug in the next five years. She speaks in an exclusive conversation with Bloomberg's Haslinda Amin. (Source: Bloomberg)

title: Chinese Bonds Draw Foreign Funds in May After a Year of Outflows
summary: Foreign investors returned to Chinese sovereign bonds in May for the first time in over a year, drawn by the market’s resilience while a brutal selloff battered global debt.

title: SpaceX Surges for Second Day to Add $412 Billion in Value
summary: SpaceX shares jumped in their second day of trading, adding to gains following a blockbuster debut that instantly vaulted it into the ranks of the world’s most valuable public companies.

title: Woodside Energy’s Louisiana LNG President Abruptly Departs
summary: The top executive at Woodside Energy Group Ltd.’s $17.5 billion liquefied natural gas project in Louisiana has departed, effective Monday, just over a year after she was hired, according to a company memo seen by Bloomberg.

title: SpaceX IPO in Focus as Iran Fears Ease | The Asia Trade 6/12/2026
summary:
"Bloomberg: The Asia Trade" brings you everything you need to know to get ahead as the trading day begins in Asia. Bloomberg TV is live from Tokyo and Sydney with Shery Ahn and Haidi Stroud-Watts, getting insight and analysis from newsmakers and industry leaders on the biggest stories shaping global markets.
 (Source: Bloomberg)
